diagrams.title: System Call Categories Overview
diagrams.description: Shows the six main categories of system calls and their interrelationships with user space and kernel space
elements: Categories: Process Control, File Management, Device Management, Information Maintenance, Communication, Protection
Return to User Space with results
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the architecture of system calls and how user programs interact with the OS kernel through well-defined categories.

diagrams.title: fork() and exec() Process Creation Flow
diagrams.description: Illustrates the sequence of fork() and exec() system calls in creating and running a new program
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how a new program is loaded and executed through the combination of fork() and exec() system calls.

diagrams.title: File Descriptor Table
diagrams.description: Shows how file descriptors work in Unix-like systems with the standard streams and opened files
elements: Each fd points to Open File Table entry
diagrams.purpose: Understanding file descriptors as the primary mechanism for accessing files and I/O streams in operating systems.

diagrams.title: Inter-Process Communication via Pipes
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how pipe(), read(), and write() system calls enable parent-child communication
elements: pipe() creates pipe [read end, write end]
Parent writes to pipe via write()
Child reads from pipe via read()
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how communication system calls create data channels between related processes.
